what: Step 1: Prepare the List of Analysis (LOA)
title: Data Analysis
text: Create load LOA file. must contain columns: Note Metadata: can include optional metadata columns sector, indicator, sub_indicator directly LOA file. extra columns automatically joined results pass names via extra_columns argument Step 2. Look examples information.
code: library(readxl) loa <- read_excel("path/to/loa.xlsx")  # Define the grouping variables (Overall must come first) grouping_variables <- c("Overall", "gender", "female_hoh")

title: Data Analysis
text: run_group_analysis_pipeline() loops every grouping variable produces single combined wide dataset.
code: library(extrar)  group_analysis <- run_group_analysis_pipeline(   dataset          = my_dataset,   loa              = loa,   group_variables  = grouping_variables,   tool_survey      = tool_survey,   tool_choices     = tool_choices,   weight_column    = "weights",   strata_column    = "sample_location",   extra_columns    = c("sector", "indicator", "sub_indicator")  # optional )  # Access the combined results group_analysis$combined_results

what: Step 3: Export a Formatted Excel Output
title: Data Analysis
text: format_my_xlsx_variable_x_group() takes combined results writes styled Excel file. insert_empty_rows = TRUE, blank separator row inserted every block related questions. empty_rows_col controls column defines grouping blank row insertion. defaults "analysis_var" (renamed "question" output), since rows given question share value.
code: format_my_xlsx_variable_x_group(   table_group_x_variable = group_analysis$combined_results,   file_path              = "output/analysis_output.xlsx",   insert_empty_rows      = TRUE,   empty_rows_col         = "analysis_var",  # groups rows by question block   overwrite              = TRUE )

text: examples LOA translates final formatted Excel output.

text: running pipeline standard analysis types extra metadata columns:     Left: Basic LOA input. Right: Resulting formatted Excel output.

title: Data Preparation & Cleaning
text: package includes secure data acquisition layer authenticate download survey data directly KoboToolbox via API. Setup Token: first time use , securely store API token system’s credential store using keyring package: Download Data: Use kobo_download_data trigger export download .xlsx file data/ folder automatically:
code: kobo_setup_token() # Prompts for token input # Downloads the latest dataset for the specified asset ID filepath <- kobo_download_data("your_kobo_asset_id_here")

what: Safely Read and Standardize Data (read_raw_data & read_loop_data)
title: Data Preparation & Cleaning
text: Instead manually mutating every single column, tools read data using original kobo_survey object auto-detect integer, decimal, date, datetime columns, fixing instantly upon import. can directly pass filepath downloaded Kobo API : read_raw_data: Standardizes main dataset, ensures UUID columns aligned, optionally adds extra dates times missing standard format. read_loop_data: Pulls roster sheets generates robust composite UUID ([row_number]_[parent_uuid]) prevent primary key merging issues loops.

title: Data Preparation & Cleaning
text: Preparing text responses cleaning log translations can extremely tedious. “” responses suite connects “” text fields main data loop datasets one centralized recode sheet:
code: # 1. Map out which 'text' questions correspond to 'other' inputs  other_labels <- get_other_labels(kobo_survey)  # 2. Match those specific choices with the dropdown elements from your Kobo tool  other_db <- get_other_db(kobo_survey, kobo_choices, other_labels)  # 3. Pull all actual raw responses, merge them, and optionally attach extra metadata columns other_responses <- prepare_other_responses(   raw_data,    other_db,    kobo_choices,    raw_loops = list(loop_data1, loop_data2), # Include loop data where necessary   extra_columns = c("enumerator_id", "location") )  # 4. Generate a heavily formatted Excel workbook complete with column tracking, colors, and dynamic dropdown logic validations save_other_responses(other_responses, save_location = "output/", other_db = other_db)

title: Download Kobo Audit Files — kobo_download_audits
text: Downloads audit log files submission Kobo server. Audit files track enumerator timing (long survey question took). Fetches audit URLs directly Kobo JSON API, works regardless main data downloaded. Already-downloaded audits skipped automatically.

title: Download Kobo Audit Files — kobo_download_audits
code: kobo_download_audits(   asset_id,   server_url = "https://kobo.impact-initiatives.org",   audit_dir = "audits/",   zip_output = TRUE )

title: Download Kobo Audit Files — kobo_download_audits
text: asset_id Kobo asset ID. server_url Kobo server URL. audit_dir Directory save downloaded audit files. zip_output TRUE, creates zip file audit folders downloading (default: TRUE).

text: Invisibly returns audit directory path.

title: Download KoBo Submission Media — kobo_download_media
text: Downloads image media attachments submission KoBo server. Files automatically renamed based XLSForm question/indicator name (e.g., photo_trash.jpg) instead keeping generic timestamps. Folders structured enumerator submission UUID (enum_uuid) maintain data tracking. Fetches data directly KoBo JSON API skips already-downloaded files automatically.

title: Download KoBo Submission Media — kobo_download_media
code: kobo_download_media(   asset_id,   server_url = "https://kobo.impact-initiatives.org",   media_dir = "images/",   enum_col = "enum",   zip_output = TRUE )

title: Download KoBo Submission Media — kobo_download_media
text: asset_id String. KoBo asset/form unique identifier (UID). server_url String. base URL KoBo server instance. Default "https://kobo.impact-initiatives.org". media_dir String. target base directory path save downloaded images. Default "images/". enum_col String. exact field/column name form tracking enumerator's identity. Default "enum". zip_output Logical. TRUE, bundles downloaded subfolders single zip archive. Default TRUE.

title: Download KoBo Submission Media — kobo_download_media
text: Invisibly returns base media directory path string.

what: Run Full Group Analysis Pipeline — run_group_analysis_pipeline
title: Run Full Group Analysis Pipeline — run_group_analysis_pipeline
text: function iterates list group variables, performs standard analysis group, adds labels, pivots results. first group list (usually 'Overall'), assigns unique UUID result row. subsequent group variables, matches results back original UUIDs using analysis_var, analysis_type, analysis_var_value. avoids need repeatedly read write Excel file loop, producing one massive dataset spanning groups.

title: Run Full Group Analysis Pipeline — run_group_analysis_pipeline
code: run_group_analysis_pipeline(   dataset,   loa,   group_variables = c("Overall"),   tool_survey,   tool_choices,   weight_column = "weights",   strata_column = "sample_location",   value_columns = c("stat", "n", "n_total"),   extra_columns = NULL )

title: Run Full Group Analysis Pipeline — run_group_analysis_pipeline
text: dataset survey dataset. loa List Analysis (LOA) data frame. typically loaded Excel file containing full list variables analyse, analysis types, metadata columns. Common optional metadata columns can passed via extra_columns include sector, indicator, subset_indicator. group_variables character vector group variables iterate . Defaults c("Overall"). tool_survey Kobo tool survey sheet. tool_choices Kobo tool choices sheet. weight_column column name survey weights. Defaults "weights". strata_column column name survey strata. Defaults "sample_location". value_columns Columns extract results. Defaults c("stat", "n", "n_total"). extra_columns Extra LOA metadata columns carry output (e.g. c("sector", "indicator", "subset_indicator")). Defaults NULL.

title: Run Full Group Analysis Pipeline — run_group_analysis_pipeline

title: extrar 2026.7.0
text: Added unit testing framework using testthat (edition 3). Enabled Markdown parsing function documentation (roxygen2). Configured permissive MIT License. Added README badges build status (R-check.yaml).

title: extrar 2026.7.0
text: Added kobo_download_data() download survey datasets securely KoboToolbox via API. Added read_raw_data() read_loop_data() standardize data types manage composite loop keys.

title: extrar 2026.7.0
text: Centralized mapped “” text inputs using prepare_other_responses(). Generated styled Excel files translation/recoding save_other_responses().

title: extrar 2026.7.0
text: Implemented run_group_analysis_pipeline() automate survey analysis across multiple grouping variables. Added format_my_xlsx_variable_x_group() export fully styled, group-aligned Excel sheets.
